授業目的: The purpose of this course is to provide students with a practical understanding of research design and methods for conducting business research.
到達目標:
Students will acquire a practical understanding of research, its structure, and processes.
 Students will be able to organize their research ideas and distinguish between good research and research that falls short of professional quality.
 Students will learn how to use AI tools responsibly and effectively to assist with research ideas and gather literature.
 Students will develop the ability to prepare a feasible and worthwhile proposal for the Individual Research course.
授業方法: In-person class  discussion that includes understanding research structure and its processes, developing research proposals and experiential learning presentations.

About 100 minutes of self-study per session

About 100 minutes of self-study per session
第7回: About 100 minutes of self-study per session
第8回: About 100 minutes of self-study per session
第9回: Primary Data through Observation, Semi-structured, In-depth and Group Interviews
About 100 minutes of self-study per session

About 100 minutes of self-study per session
第11回: About 100 minutes of self-study per session
第12回: About 100 minutes of self-study per session

トピック.授業外学習2: This course is mainly for students who major in management.

教科書/Required texts0:
Mark Saunders, Philip Lewis & Andrian Thornhill, Research Methods for Business Students, 5th Edition, Prentice-Hall, July 2009. 

A selected article will be discussed at the beginning of the course to familiarize students with research structure and contents. Students are then expected to choose and review two articles relevant to their research interest in class discussions.
参考文献・資料/Reference books0: Paul Leedy, and Jeanne Ormrod, Practical Research:Planning and Design, 8th Edition, Pearson Prentice Hall, 2005.
